findings.issue: Export handler null-name path (default identity export) has zero test coverage. Only the named-export and not-found paths are tested. Lines 27-33 (load all, find default, return 404 if no default) are untested.
findings.impact: If the default-export path returned the wrong identity or failed to find the default, no test would catch it.

findings.issue: Create_EmptyOrWhitespaceName_ReturnsError (line 145-147): whitespace name case only checks Success==false, does NOT check error key. Could return wrong error type and test would still pass.
findings.impact: If whitespace validation returned 'DuplicateName' instead of 'ValidationError', test would pass as false green.

findings.issue: SetDefault_ArchivedOrMissing_ReturnsError (line 357-359): missing-identity case only checks Success==false, does NOT check error key. Different from archived case which correctly checks 'ArchivedIdentity'.
findings.impact: If missing identity returned 'ArchivedIdentity' instead of 'NotFound', test would still pass.

findings.issue: All 10 PLang test goals are stubs ('throw not implemented'). Zero PLang pipeline coverage for the identity module.
findings.impact: No validation that the builder can map natural language identity steps to the correct handlers. Builder bugs (wrong module, missing params) would be invisible.
findings.suggestion: Implement at minimum: IdentityCreate, IdentityGetByName, IdentityAutoCreate, IdentityExport PLang tests. These cover the primary use cases.

findings.issue: types.cs has 77% line / 52% branch coverage. The Deserialize() method's dictionary path and exception-swallowing catch block are untested. LoadAllAsync error path (returns empty list on failure) is also untested.
findings.impact: Silent deserialization failures could produce null identities that cause NullReferenceException downstream. The catch-all exception handler in Deserialize() hides real errors.
findings.suggestion: Add test for Deserialize with malformed dictionary input to verify null return. Consider whether the catch-all in Deserialize is appropriate or should log.

findings.issue: No test for case-insensitive name collision on rename. Code uses OrdinalIgnoreCase for uniqueness (correct), but no test verifies rename('alice', 'ALICE') would fail against existing 'Alice'.
findings.impact: If case-insensitive comparison were accidentally removed, no test would catch the regression.

findings.issue: No test for case-insensitive name collision on create. Same reasoning as finding #6.
findings.impact: If uniqueness check changed to case-sensitive, no test would catch the regression.

findings.issue: DotNavigation_Created_ReturnsCreated only checks type is DateTime, not the actual value. Could return DateTime.MinValue or any DateTime and test would pass.
findings.impact: Low — Created is set in constructor, unlikely to be wrong. But weakens confidence.
findings.suggestion: Assert the actual DateTime value matches the one set in CreateTestIdentity().

summary: All 1645 C# tests pass. Coverage is excellent on most handlers (100% line) but export.cs default path is untested (60% line, 38% branch). Two weak assertions miss error key checks on error paths (whitespace create, missing setDefault). All 10 PLang tests are unimplemented stubs. Three major findings require fixes: export default path coverage, two weak assertion gaps, and PLang test stubs.
